tst_2024: In consideration of the question "Who am I?" with 20 responses, the trigger has been pulled to launch into an exended stream of consciousness semi-rant of a decidly existentialist perspective. However, I shall refrain. I'm a 76 YO correctly gender-assigned woman. I feel & usually thing like maybe a 22-28 YO. 'm not counting the number of anawers-we'll see what shakes out. I was born/raised in urban-ish NJ across from NYC. Parents were from the south/Virgina (M) and Hell's Kitchen D). Grew up with the subtle vibe of re-fighting the civil war. Dad wanted a son; I was what he got. Worked in my favor, as I despised the white gloves on Sunday and "girls must, girls should, girls can't" of the entire marernal side of the family tree. I learned how to navigate liefe and survive, I learned skills and interesting, useful stuff, and I got to tag-along on Boy Scout outings & camping w/my dad. Wanted to be an artitst-didn't work out; got admitted to Cooper Union (free tuition upon qualification) but the slot yanked out from under me last minute, due to a quota system the replaced me with the daughter of a Columbia dean. Left me bitter. Spent YAhood in NY in the 60's. Not writng a novel, so we'll skip the juicy, and gory, details. I am a product of those years more than any other. Still truckin', as they say, after a life of multiple unrelated traumas, a successful career, 2 decent love mates, 1 child, and (never mind-not going down the rabbit hole. Will try to count to see if I have 20 responses yet.) Lost track after 13-I'm assuming I have complied. At base, I am intelligent, curious, creative, pessimistic and cynical, NY-style sorta-kinda abrupt (not a good fit for the years I spent living in VA-now I'm in upper NE and that works better for all conserned. Plus, love snow.
